description: Same team meeting where one has location and other doesn't - should match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Q2 Planning Session
- Start: 2026-03-30T13:00:00
- End: 2026-03-30T15:00:00
- Location: Conference Room B
- Description: Quarterly planning for engineering team. Bring your roadmap ideas.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Coffee Chat with Sarah
   - Start: 2026-03-30T10:00:00
   - End: 2026-03-30T10:30:00
   - Location: Starbucks
   - Description: Catch up

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Engineering Q2 Planning
   - Start: 2026-03-30T13:00:00
   - End: 2026-03-30T15:00:00
   - Location: 
   - Description: Planning session for second quarter. All team members required.

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Yoga Class
   - Start: 2026-03-30T18:00:00
   - End: 2026-03-30T19:00:00
   - Location: Zen Studio
   - Description: Evening yoga

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.
